sections.paragraph:
ἀφρίζω (ἀφρός; Soph., El. 719 horses; Diod S 3, 10, 5 elephants; Athen. 11, 43 p. 472a; TestSol 12:2) to produce a foam, foam (at the mouth) of a sick person, prob. in an epileptic seizure Mk 9:18, 20.—DELG s.v. ἀφρός.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

prob. prob. = probable, probably

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 159
